title: Knowing prehistory
description: Test your knowledge of archaeological terms!
topic_path: The origin of humans and early human societies

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why is it difficult for historians to study early human societies?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Artifacts and fossils are the primary sources of information about early human societies.
Early human societies that we consider a part of "prehistory"  hadn't developed writing.

The correct answer is:

**Before the advent of writing, historians had fewer and less detailed resources to work with**.

choices.content: Written records from that time are indecipherable or meaningless
There are no surviving artifacts or fossils from that time
Before the advent of writing, historians had fewer and less detailed resources to work with

assessment_items.question_content:
What's the difference between **carbon dating** and **stratigraphic dating**?



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Stratigraphic dating relies on the layers of earth surrounding fossils or artifacts
Carbon dating places fossils or artifacts in time by measuring their radiocarbon content.

The correct answer is:

**Carbon dating places fossils or artifacts in time by measuring their radiocarbon content; stratigraphic dating relies on the layers of earth surrounding those fossils or artifacts**.

choices.content: Carbon dating places fossils or artifacts in time by measuring their radiocarbon content; stratigraphic dating relies on the layers of earth surrounding those fossils or artifacts
Stratigraphic dating uses the content of the fossils or artifacts themselves to place them in time; carbon dating relies on the layers of earth surrounding fossils or artifacts
Stratigraphic dating is a type of radioactive dating that only applies to matter which was once living
